GenericErrorResponseModel.description: Generic error response. The message will provide more details about the error.

httpCode.description: The HTTP status code of the error response.

httpMessage.description: The HTTP status message of the error response.

message.description: A human-readable error message, useful when no message translations are available

messageTranslations.description: A map of language codes to human-readable error messages, for clients that can use them.

CentralAuthSessionProvider-Session.description:
MediaWiki Session ID cookie.
Note: Write operations using this method also require a CSRF token to be submitted in the request body.
CentralAuthSessionProvider-Session.name: mediawikiwikiSession

CentralAuthSessionProvider-Token.description:
MediaWiki User Token cookie.
Required for persistent session verification.
CentralAuthSessionProvider-Token.name: mediawikiwikiToken

CentralAuthSessionProvider-UserID.description:
MediaWiki User ID cookie.
Required alongside the session ID.
CentralAuthSessionProvider-UserID.name: mediawikiwikiUserID

put.description: Edits a page based on the page's latest revision identifier, or creates a page if no revision identifier is given. If there is a merge conflict, the API attempts to resolve the conflict automatically. If the edit conflict cannot be resolved, the API returns a 409 error.

get.description: Returns information about a page.

parameters.description: Page title. To format the title, replace spaces with underscores, and then URL-encode the result (example: Steller%27s_jay). In the API sandbox, replace spaces with underscores, but do not URL-encode (example: Steller's_jay).
parameters.name: title

parameters.description: Specifies if redirects should be followed, such as following common page aliases or if a page has moved. Default is set to `true`.
parameters.name: redirect

html_url.description: API route to fetch the content of the page in HTML

id.description: Revision identifier for the latest revision

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

get.description: Returns information about the latest revisions to a page, in segments of 20 revisions, starting with the latest revision. The response includes URLs to get the next oldest, next newest, and latest revision segments, letting you scroll through page history.

parameters.description: Page title. To format the title, replace spaces with underscores, and then URL-encode the result (example: Steller%27s_jay). In the API sandbox, replace spaces with underscores, but do not URL-encode (example: Steller's_jay).
parameters.name: title

parameters.description: Accepts a revision ID. Returns the next 20 revisions older than the given revision ID.
parameters.name: older_than

parameters.description: Accepts a revision ID. Returns the next 20 revisions newer than the given revision ID.
parameters.name: newer_than

parameters.description: Use a filter to return only revisions tagged as (reverted, anonymous, bot, minor). Accepts one filter per request.
parameters.name: filter

latest.description: URL to the latest revision of the page

newer.description: If available, URL to get the following revisions

older.description: If available, URL to get the prior revisions

revisions.description: List of revisions of the page

comment.description: The comment the author associated with the revision

delta.description: The difference in size compared to the previous revision.

minor.description: Whether or not this is a minor edit

size.description: Size of this revision in bytes

user.description: Information about the user who made the revision

id.description: User identifier, or null for anonymous users

name.description: Username, or originating IP address for anonymous users

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

get.description: Returns data about a page's history, such as the number of edits made by different types of users. For more data about how readers and editors interact with Wikimedia projects, see the [Analytics API](https://doc.wikimedia.org/generated-data-platform/aqs/analytics-api/).

parameters.description: Page title. To format the title, replace spaces with underscores, and then URL-encode the result (example: Steller%27s_jay). In the API sandbox, replace spaces with underscores, but do not URL-encode (example: Steller's_jay).
parameters.name: title

parameters.description: Type of count (anonymous, bot, editors, edits, minor, reverted)
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Restricts the count to between two revisions, specified by revision ID. Must be used with the "to" parameter.
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: Restricts the count to between two revisions, specified by revision ID. Must be used with the "from" parameter.
parameters.name: to

count.description: Number of edit counts of the specified type

limit.description: Whether the actual edit count exceeded the allowed limit for that type

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

Link.description: Provides a means for serializing one or more links in HTTP headers. This allows the server to point a client to another resource containing metadata about the requested resource.

get.description: Returns the lint errors for a page.

parameters.description: Page title. To format the title, replace spaces with underscores, and then URL-encode the result (example: Steller%27s_jay). In the API sandbox, replace spaces with underscores, but do not URL-encode (example: Steller's_jay).

parameters.name: redirect

dsr.description: Information about the relevant DOM source range. The array will always contain, in order: starting position (inclusive), ending position (exclusive), width of the opening tag, and width of the closing tag. In some cases, the array will contain two more elements, in order: width of whitespace characters between the opening tag and first child, and width of whitespace characters between the last child and closing tag. All of these positions and widths are measured in UTF-8 bytes.

params.description: Additional information specific to the lint error type

multiPartTemplateBlock.description: Whether the template spans multiple template blocks

templateInfo.description: Information about which template was being processed

html.description: Latest page content in HTML, following the HTML specification

id.description: Revision identifier for the latest revision

title.description: Name of the applicable license based on the $wgRightsText setting

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

get.description: Returns information that lets you display a line-by-line comparison of two revisions. Only text-based wiki pages can be compared.

parameters.description: Revision used as the base for comparison
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: Revision identifier
parameters.name: to

highlightRanges.description: Array of objects that indicate where and in what style text should be highlighted to visually represent changes

length.description: Length of the highlighted section, in bytes

start.description: Where the highlighted text should start, in the number of bytes from the beginning of the line

type.description: Type of highlight: 0 indicates an addition, 1 indicates a deletion

lineNumber.description: Line number of the change based on the `to` revision

moveInfo.description: Visual indicators to use when a paragraph's location differs between the two revisions. moveInfo objects occur in pairs within the diff.

id.description: Identifier of the paragraph described by the diff object

linkDirection.description: Visual indicator of the relationship between the two locations. Use this property to display an arrow icon within the diff. 0 indicates that the linkId paragraph is lower on the page than the id paragraph. 1 indicates that the linkId paragraph is higher on the page than the id paragraph.

linkId.description: Identifier of the corresponding paragraph. For type 4, the location in the `to` revision. For type 5, the location in the `from` revision.

offset.description: Location of the line in bytes from the beginning of the page

from.description: First byte of the line in the `from` revision, or null if the line doesn't exist

to.description: First byte of the line in the `to` revision, or null if the line doesn't exist

text.description: Text of the line, including content from both revisions. For a line containing text that differs between the two revisions, use highlightRanges to visually indicate added and removed text. For a line containing a new line, the API returns "" (empty string).
text.example: * {{Visible anchor|Saturn|text=[[Saturn]]}} (9.08–10.12 AU)<ref name="nasa-factsheet" group="D" /> has a distinctive visible [[Rings of Saturn|ring system]] orbiting around its equator composed of small ice and rock particles. Like Jupiter, it is mostly made of hydrogen and helium.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 August 2021 |title=In Depth: Saturn |url=https://solarsystem.nasa.gov/planets/saturn/in-depth |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180224210031/https://solarsystem.nasa.gov/planets/saturn/in-depth |archive-date=24 February 2018 |access-date=31 March 2022 |website=NASA Science: Solar System Exploration}}</ref> At its north and south poles, Saturn has peculiar [[Saturn's hexagon|hexagon-shaped storms]] larger than the diameter of Earth. [[Magnetosphere of Saturn|Saturn has a magnetosphere]] capable of producing weak auroras. {{As of|2026}}, Saturn has [[Moons of Saturn|292285 confirmed satellites]], grouped into:

type.description:
Type of change, either:
  • `0`: Same content in both revisions, included to provide context when viewing the diff. The API returns up to two context lines around each change.
  • `1`: Line included in the `to` revision but not in the `from` revision
  • `2`: Line included in the `from` revision but not in the `to` revision
  • `3`: Line containing text that differs between the two revisions
  • `4`: Location of moved content in the `from` revision
  • `5`: Location of moved content in the `to` revision. This type can also include word-level differences between the two revisions.

from.description: Revision used as the base for comparison

sections.description: Preprocessor sections for the revision, without template expansion

heading.description: Contents of the heading line in source format

offset.description: Location of the section, in bytes from the beginning of the page

slot_role.description: Role for the slot being compared (always 'main')

to.description: Revision compared to the base revision

sections.description: Preprocessor sections for the revision, without template expansion

heading.description: Contents of the heading line in source format

offset.description: Location of the section, in bytes from the beginning of the page

slot_role.description: Role for the slot being compared (always 'main')

user_id.description: Included only if the x-restbase-compat header is used to activate RESTBase compatibility

user_text.description: Included only if the x-restbase-compat header is used to activate RESTBase compatibility

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

get.description: Returns the content of a revision in HTML.

parameters.description: Revision identifier
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: HTML output renderer stash
parameters.name: stash

parameters.description: HTML output renderer flavor
parameters.name: flavor

parameters.description: The natural language and locale that the client prefers.
parameters.name: Accept-Language

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

Content-Type.description: Indicates the original media type of a resource before any content encoding is applied.

get.description: Returns the lint errors for a revision.

parameters.description: Revision identifier
parameters.name: id

dsr.description: Information about the relevant DOM source range. The array will always contain, in order: starting position (inclusive), ending position (exclusive), width of the opening tag, and width of the closing tag. In some cases, the array will contain two more elements, in order: width of whitespace characters between the opening tag and first child, and width of whitespace characters between the last child and closing tag. All of these positions and widths are measured in UTF-8 bytes.

params.description: Additional information specific to the lint error type

multiPartTemplateBlock.description: Whether the template spans multiple template blocks

templateInfo.description: Information about which template was being processed

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

get.description: Returns information about a revision, including the revision content in HTML.

parameters.description: Revision identifier
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: HTML output renderer stash
parameters.name: stash

parameters.description: HTML output renderer flavor
parameters.name: flavor

parameters.description: The natural language and locale that the client prefers.
parameters.name: Accept-Language

comment.description: The comment the author associated with the revision

delta.description: The difference in size compared to the previous revision.

html.description: Revision content in HTML, following the HTML specification

title.description: Name of the applicable license based on the $wgRightsText setting

url.description: URL of the applicable license based on the $wgRightsUrl setting

minor.description: Whether or not this is a minor edit

page.description: The page the revision belongs to

size.description: Size of this revision in bytes

id.description: User identifier, or null for anonymous users

name.description: Username, or originating IP address for anonymous users

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

Content-Type.description: Indicates the original media type of a resource before any content encoding is applied.

get.description: Returns an [OpenSearch description document](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/XML/Guides/OpenSearch).

parameters.description: Open search description ctype
parameters.name: ctype

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

Content-Type.description: Indicates the original media type of a resource before any content encoding is applied.

Expires.description: Contains the date and time after which the response is considered expired in the context of HTTP caching.

get.description: Lists pages matching the given search terms.

parameters.description: Search terms
parameters.name: q

parameters.description: Maximum number of search results to return, between 1 and 100. Default: 50
parameters.name: limit

pages.description: Video game played on a mobile device
pages.excerpt: PDA to handheld game console, portable media player or graphing calculator
pages.title: Mobile game

anchor.description: Section of the target page, if the search term matched a redirect to a specific section, in human-readable format

description.description: Short summary of the page topic or null if no summary exists.
description.example: Video game played on a mobile device

excerpt.description: For /search/page, a short sample of page content with search terms tagged with `<span class="searchmatch">`. Excerpts may end mid-sentence. For /search/title, the page title in reading-friendly format
excerpt.example: PDA to handheld game console, portable media player or graphing calculator, with and without network availability. The earliest known game on a mobile phone

matched_title.description: Title of the page redirected from, if the search term matched a redirect page, or else null

thumbnail.description: Information about the thumbnail image for the page, or null if no thumbnail exists.

duration.description: The length of the video, audio, or multimedia file or null for other media types

height.description: Maximum recommended image height in pixels or null if not available

url.description: URL to download the file. URL may start with https or //.

width.description: Maximum recommended image width in pixels or null if not available

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

get.description: Lists pages with titles that begin with the given search terms. You can use this endpoint as an autocomplete search that suggests relevant pages by title.

parameters.description: Search terms
parameters.name: q

parameters.description: Maximum number of search results to return, between 1 and 100. Default: 50
parameters.name: limit

pages.description: Video game played on a mobile device
pages.excerpt: PDA to handheld game console, portable media player or graphing calculator
pages.title: Mobile game

anchor.description: Section of the target page, if the search term matched a redirect to a specific section, in human-readable format

description.description: Short summary of the page topic or null if no summary exists.
description.example: Video game played on a mobile device

excerpt.description: For /search/page, a short sample of page content with search terms tagged with `<span class="searchmatch">`. Excerpts may end mid-sentence. For /search/title, the page title in reading-friendly format
excerpt.example: PDA to handheld game console, portable media player or graphing calculator, with and without network availability. The earliest known game on a mobile phone

matched_title.description: Title of the page redirected from, if the search term matched a redirect page, or else null

thumbnail.description: Information about the thumbnail image for the page, or null if no thumbnail exists.

duration.description: The length of the video, audio, or multimedia file or null for other media types

height.description: Maximum recommended image height in pixels or null if not available

url.description: URL to download the file. URL may start with https or //.

width.description: Maximum recommended image width in pixels or null if not available

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.

parameters.description: Indicates the original media type of a resource before any content encoding is applied.
parameters.name: Content-Type

parameters.description: The natural language and locale that the client prefers.
parameters.name: Accept-Language

parameters.description: Allow servers to store state information on the client's browser, enabling features like session management, personalization, and tracking.
parameters.name: Cookie

parameters.description: Provides credentials that authenticate a user agent with a server, allowing access to protected resources.
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: Enables tracing and correlating individual HTTP requests as they flow through distributed systems, such as microservices, API gateways, and load balancer.
parameters.name: X-Request-Id

parameters.description: Sends a characteristic string to web servers, allowing them to identify the application, operating system, vendor, and/or version of the requesting client software.
parameters.name: User-Agent

parameters.description: Makes a request conditional, ensuring the requested action (like an update or deletion) is only performed if the resource on the server matches a specific version (identified by its ETag) provided by the client.
parameters.name: If-Match

Cache-Control.description: Dictates caching behavior for a browser, letting it know how often to refresh locally stored resources.
